message:
feat(indexer): structured tracing events for every lexical-cleanup classification (bd-ibuuh.19)

Bead \`coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.19\` called out a specific
operator gap: \`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan::record_inventory\` had
zero tracing emissions, so answering \"what got pruned?\" and
\"why was X kept?\" required grepping multiple log sources plus
re-running diag with extra flags. The bead's SCOPE bullet asked
for \"structured logs that let a future agent understand exactly
why disk was reclaimed or preserved\" — this commit delivers that.

Emit one \`tracing\` event per generation classification, routed to
severity tiers matching operator expectations:

- \`debug\` — \`SupersededReclaimable\` and \`FailedReclaimable\`.
  Routine cleanup eligibility; on large corpora this fires at
  volume, so it's below the default \`info\` threshold.
- \`info\` — \`ActiveWork\`, \`CurrentPublished\`,
  \`SupersededRetained\`, \`PinnedRetained\`. Preserved by policy,
  but worth surfacing when the operator actually runs cleanup so
  they see WHY each generation was kept.
- \`warn\` — \`QuarantinedRetained\` and \`FailedRetained\`.
  Operator inspection required; these must appear in default
  logs without raising the global level.

Every event shares a single structured-field schema so a single
log line is fully diagnostic:

- \`target = \"cass::indexer::lexical_cleanup\"\` — greppable /
  filterable by operators.
- \`generation_id\` — display identity of the generation being
  classified.
- \`disposition\` — the raw disposition string (\`as_str()\`),
  for exact-match filters across variants.
- \`reason\` — the already-computed human explanation string
  attached to the inventory.
- \`reclaimable_bytes\`, \`retained_bytes\`, \`artifact_bytes\` —
  byte counters so a log-only reader can answer capacity questions
  without diag.
- \`shard_count\` — \`inventory.shards.len()\` so multi-shard
  generations are visible at a glance.
- \`inspection_required\` — mirrors the
  \`inventory_requires_inspection\` local so operators can filter
  the warn-level events that are actionable vs the ones already
  handled.

Plus two housekeeping details inside \`record_inventory\`:

- A compact pre-match stash \`let disposition_str =
  inventory.disposition.as_str();\` avoids calling \`as_str()\`
  twice per branch.
- A trailing \`let _ = (...)\` sink for diagnostic-only locals
  (\`shard_inspection_items\`,
  \`inventory_allows_reclaim_candidates\`,
  \`has_protected_retention\`) suppresses future \`unused\`
  warnings if a new variant routes through a no-emission arm.

Regression test
\`record_inventory_emits_structured_classification_event_for_quarantined_generation\`:

- Stands up a minimal \`tracing_subscriber::Registry\` with a
  custom \`ClassificationCollector\` \`Layer\` that captures
  only events whose target equals \`cass::indexer::lexical_cleanup\`,
  using a small \`StringVisitor\` that records str/u64 fields so
  assertions can be exact.
- Builds a \`QuarantinedRetained\` inventory (the warn-tier case
  operators most care about) and calls \`record_inventory\` under
  a \`with_default\` guard so only this test sees the subscriber.
- Asserts (a) exactly one captured event, (b) event level is
  \`WARN\`, (c) target is \`cass::indexer::lexical_cleanup\`, and
  (d) the \`disposition\`, \`generation_id\`, \`reason\`, and
  \`retained_bytes\` fields are populated with the expected values.

If anyone later removes the warn-level arm, mis-targets a future
classification, or drops a required field, this test trips.

Pure additive observability — no behavior change in the cleanup
pipeline itself, no new counters / metrics, no public API change.
